title: Deterministic Tests, Fault Injection, Contract Evidence, and Observability · Browser and Node… · Contract Lens: Contract Changed Case Tests
summary: A cross-runtime SDK needs deterministic fetch fakes, local integration tests, abort and truncation faults, contract fixtures, and Web Performance telemetry. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts contract changed case tests increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch fault-injected client verification harness. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Verify documented provider and consumer contracts against changed schemas, status codes, and version boundaries. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Use a local test server for protocol integration while controlling redirects, delays, truncation, TLS, and cleanup. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
